title: RACF: Authorization Plane Analysis
description: Examine how RACF evaluates access. Resource profiles, identity footprint, policy enforcement gaps.

steps.title: RACF Overview
steps.screen:
RACF — Resource Access Control Facility

RACF is the z/OS security manager.
It controls:
- Who can log on (user profiles)
- What they can access (resource profiles)
- What they can do (authority levels)

All access decisions flow through RACF.
steps.expected: Understand RACF as the authorization plane for z/OS.
steps.hint: RACF is not a firewall — it is an identity-bound authorization engine.

steps.title: List User Profile
steps.screen:
TSO COMMAND: LU HERC01

USER=HERC01  NAME=HERCULES USER  OWNER=SYS1
CREATED=99.001  DEFAULT-GROUP=SYS1  PASSDATE=00.000
PASS-INTERVAL=30  PHRASEDATE=N/A  ATTRIBUTES=SPECIAL
REVOKECOUNT=00  NOPASSWORD  NOOIDCARD

GROUP=SYS1    AUTH=USE      CONNECT-OWNER=SYS1
steps.expected: HERC01 has SPECIAL attribute — equivalent to root on z/OS.
steps.hint: SPECIAL = RACF administrator. This user can bypass all resource checks.

steps.title: List Group Members
steps.screen:
TSO COMMAND: LG SYS1

GROUP=SYS1    OWNER=SYS1    SUPGROUP=
INSTALLATION DATA=
TERMINAL ACCESS ALLOWED

USER       AUTH      CONNECT-OWNER
HERC01     USE       SYS1
HERC02     USE       SYS1
IBMUSER    SPECIAL   SYS1
steps.expected: SYS1 group contains privileged users including IBMUSER with SPECIAL.
steps.hint: IBMUSER is the default superuser — always check if it exists and is active.

steps.title: Check Dataset Profile
steps.screen:
TSO COMMAND: LISTDSD DATASET('SYS1.PARMLIB') ALL

DATASET NAME: SYS1.PARMLIB
LEVEL  OWNER  UNIVERSAL ACCESS  YOUR ACCESS  WARNING
  00   SYS1   NONE              ALTER        NO

USER      ACCESS   ACCESS COUNT
HERC01    ALTER    000003
SYS1      ALTER    000001
steps.expected: SYS1.PARMLIB has UACC=NONE but HERC01 has ALTER access.
steps.hint: ALTER on PARMLIB = full system control. This is the highest-value target.

steps.title: Authority Evaluation Gap
steps.screen:
RACF Authority Evaluation:

1. Check user's SPECIAL/OPERATIONS attributes
2. Check group authority (CONNECT auth)
3. Check resource profile UACC
4. Check user-specific ACL entry
5. Check group-specific ACL entry

Gap: If UACC=READ on sensitive datasets,
ANY authenticated user can read them.
steps.expected: Understand the RACF access decision chain.
steps.hint: UACC (Universal Access) applies to ALL authenticated users — a common misconfiguration.
